statement.markdown:
### Problem Description:
Given an array of integers containing only duplicates as well as regular numbers, remove contiguous duplicate elements and replace them with a single -1 element. Note that the input array might be empty.
statement.inputFormat: The first line of input will contain 1 integer, the size of the array. The second line of input will be the array itself, each element separated by a space.
statement.outputFormat: The output should be the updated array in the same format as the input, but containing only non-duplicate elements. If the array is empty, output an empty array.
statement.notes: The -1 element should replace all contiguous duplicates in the array. If there are no duplicates, the array should remain unchanged.

examples.explanation: Replace all contiguous 5s with a single -1.

hints.text: First, iterate over the input array to keep track of indices with non-duplicate elements.
Then, replace contiguous duplicate elements with a single -1 in the array.

editorial.title: Editorial
editorial.intuition: Iterate through the array and identify non-duplicate elements.
editorial.bruteForce: Brute force by checking each element against the entire array.
editorial.betterSolution: Use a two-pointer approach to track the next non-duplicate element.
editorial.optimalSolution: Use the two-pointer approach and replace duplicates with a single -1.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The two-pointer approach ensures that we correctly remove contiguous duplicates and replace them with -1.
editorial.dryRun: Start by iterating through the array and identifying the first non-duplicate element.
commonMistakes: Failing to correctly track the next non-duplicate element.
Not replacing contiguous duplicates with a single -1.
